message:
security: prevent command whitelist bypass via path injection

The previous isCommandAllowed() extracted the basename from paths,
which meant "./echo" (a user-created script) would pass because
"echo" is whitelisted.

Now rejects any command containing "/" or "\" characters, requiring
bare command names that rely on PATH resolution. This prevents:
- ./malicious_script_named_echo
- /path/to/attacker_controlled/bash
- relative/path/to/python3

Commands must now be bare names like "echo", "python3", "bash".

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
